prompt: The method should return the maximum of three integers but sometimes returns the wrong value. What is the bug?
choices: The method should use Math.max instead of comparison operators
Using & instead of && prevents short-circuit evaluation; both sides are always evaluated which can cause issues with side effects, though the logic result is the same
The return c statement is never reached when a equals b
int parameters should be Integer to handle null comparisons
explanation: The bitwise & operator evaluates both operands unconditionally, unlike the logical && which short-circuits. While the result is the same for boolean expressions with no side effects, using & is a common bug source when the right-hand side has side effects or can throw. The idiomatic fix is &&.
